categories.name: Emergency Symptoms (Immediate Action Required)
symptoms: Unable to speak in full sentences
Stroke symptoms (e.g., facial drooping, slurred speech, sudden weakness or numbness of the face, arms, or legs)
Severe allergic reactions (difficulty breathing, swelling of the face, lips, and throat)
Major trauma (e.g., fall >10 feet or high-speed motor vehicle collision)
Ingested foreign body (i.e. swallowing a bead, coin, etc.)
Sudden confusion or mental status change
Severe headache - new onset - the worst headache of their life
Bleeding in pregnant or postpartum women
New onset dizziness in Adults over 65

categories.name: Urgent Symptoms (Same-Day Appointment)
categories.action: If none is available, contact/leave a message for the triage nurse.
symptoms: Any child with fever (under 7 years old) (measured or suggestive fever)
Acute abdominal pain with either of those symptoms: (nausea, fever, vomiting, loss of appetite, unable to keep food down, blood in stool, blood in urine)
Moderate asthma or COPD exacerbation (shortness of breath, cough, wheezing)
New-onset severe headache (with or without blurred vision, vomiting)
UTI symptoms in adults younger than 65 with flank pain or fever
Testicular pain in those under 18 years old
Concerns for blood clots in legs (swollen calf, redness, pain)
Acute back pain with numbness and tingling in lower legs

categories.name: Moderate Concerns (Appointment Within 48-72 Hours)
categories.action: Schedule an appointment (first try with their PCP; if none available book with another provider in the same clinic). As long as it is within 72 hours, no note to the triage nurse is needed. If an appointment cannot be found within a 72 hour window, send a TE to the triage nurse indicating symptoms indicate an appt within 72 hrs (per protocol), but none are available.
symptoms: Persistent cough with or without fever (no shortness of breath or wheezing)
UTI symptoms (burning on urination, urinary frequency without fever or flank pain)
Rash (insect bites, tick bites, poison ivy, concerns for shingles, hives)
Acute back pain without numbness or tingling (2 to 3 days)
Nausea or vomiting lasting less than 3 days; able to eat or drink
